text.tspan: Share of population in extreme poverty vs. GDP per capita, 1820 to 2000
On the vertical axis is the share of population with a National Accounts income lower than $5 per day, measure in
2011 international-$ (see Sources tab for details). Countries towards the bottom left corner saw economic growth
that was shared more equally – they reduced extreme poverty to low levels at a lower GDP per capita.
Source: OWID based on Maddison Project Database 2020 and van Zanden et al. (2014), Population (Gapminder, HYDE(2016) & UN (2019)),
